message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add dry_run option to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, tests in Tests/test_image.py. Please propose your files - if you also need to touch these two files, let's split precisely (e.g. by function/line range) or coordinate merge order.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() for auto-backup before overwrite. I need to edit Image.py (save method) and test_image.py. What files does your feature touch? If you also need Image.py, let's figure out disjoint regions or one of us adapts.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features (dry_run vs backup/backup_suffix) are independent params on the same Image.save() method, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible - we both need Image.py and test_image.py. Let's use region-level disjointness instead: (1) In Image.py save(), I'll add a 'dry_run' kwarg to the signature and a small early-return/no-write branch near the end of the file-writing logic only; please add your 'backup'/'backup_suffix' kwarg + backup-copy logic as a separate, clearly delimited block (e.g. right before the actual open/write call) so our diffs don't overlap on the same lines. (2) In test_image.py, I'll add new test functions named test_dry_run* only; please use test_backup* or similarly distinct names so our test additions don't collide. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT if so, or propose exact insertion points.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both touch save() in the same file, so let's split by region instead of by file. My planned edits: (1) add 'import shutil' near top imports (~line 39, alongside other stdlib imports), (2) inside save(), right after the existing line 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' (~line 2546) add two new pop lines: backup = params.pop("backup", False); backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak"), (3) inside the 'if open_fp:' block, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' (~line 2563), add an 'if not created and backup: shutil.copy2(filename, str(filename) + backup_suffix)' block BEFORE the file is opened for writing, (4) docstring additions for :param backup: and :param backup_suffix:. I will NOT touch the try/except save_handler block or the params.get('append'...) logic. Where exactly does your dry_run logic need to hook in (skip save_handler call? skip file open?) so we don't collide on the same lines? Please give me your precise insertion points.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Accepted. Confirming my precise insertion points so we don't overlap: (1) 'import shutil' near top imports ~line 39. (2) In save(), right after 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' I add 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")' - these are separate new lines, not touching your signature edit. (3) Right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' inside the 'if open_fp:' block, I add a small 'if not created and backup: shutil.copy2(...)' block, placed BEFORE the builtins.open(...) calls - i.e. before any write/truncate happens. I will not touch the try/except save_handler block, the append logic, or your dry_run early-return. (4) docstring: I'll add :param backup: and :param backup_suffix: lines near the existing :param params: docs, not touching other doc lines. (5) Tests: I'll only add test_backup* functions in test_image.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. Precise hooks for my dry_run feature so we don't overlap: (1) pop the param early and separately from your save_all-adjacent pops - I'll add 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after 'preinit()' (~line 2529), well before the save_all line you're touching. (2) Docstring: I'll add ':param dry_run:' right before the existing ':param params:' line (~line 2493) - distinct from wherever you add :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: (suggest you add yours right after :param format: block or near :returns:, so ours don't land on the same line). (3) For the open_fp/backup block: I'll restructure as 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: <your created/backup logic + file open> else: fp = cast(...)' - i.e. your backup copy logic stays nested exactly where you planned (right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)', before builtins.open calls), just now under 'elif open_fp' instead of 'if open_fp'. (4) I'll also guard the exception-handler's fp.close()/os.remove and the final fp.close() with 'and not dry_run' (dry_run never opens/creates the real file, so nothing to close/remove there - your backup/remove-on-exception logic for real writes is unaffected). This keeps your backup logic block fully intact, just under the renamed elif. ACCEPT from my side - let me know if that works for you.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementation complete. Final diff touches: (1) 'import shutil' at top, (2) docstring :param backup:/:param backup_suffix: lines right before ':returns: None', (3) two new pop() lines right after 'save_all = params.pop(...)', (4) a small 4-line backup-copy block right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' and before the params.get('append'...) branch, (5) new test functions test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_no_backup_by_default inserted right after test_readonly_save. All 196 existing tests + my 4 new ones pass. Wrote patch.txt. Should be fully disjoint from your dry_run signature/early-return changes and test_dry_run* functions. Let me know if you hit any conflicts merging.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished implementing dry_run. In Image.py: added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after preinit() (separate line from your save_all-adjacent pops), added a :param dry_run: docstring line right before :param params:, and changed 'if open_fp:' to 'elif open_fp:' preceded by 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' - your created/backup logic block stays intact under the elif, just renamed from if. Also guarded the exception-handler fp.close() and final fp.close() with 'and not dry_run'. Added 3 new tests (test_dry_run, test_dry_run_existing_file_untouched, test_dry_run_raises_for_invalid_save) - no test_backup* names used. Full test_image.py + test_file_png.py + test_file_jpeg.py suite passes (357 passed). All done on my end.
